title: The inland-China cervical cancer rise
text: Jiashan County women +738 %, Qidong +273 %, Harbin Nangang +271 %, Wuhan +185 %, Zhongshan +182 %, Beijing +167 %. All rising while coastal Shanghai and the rest of East Asia fall. This is the pattern that prompted the project; a focused figure showing the coastal-fall / inland-rise duality is the core story. See [Cervix uteri](cancer.html#code=32).
notes: SMALL_N id=415600799 sx=2 min cases=14; POP_WARN id=415600199 vol=10

title: Korea's thyroid-cancer screening bubble, caught rising and falling in one dataset
text: Daegu women peaked at 119.7 per 100 000 in vol XI (2008–12), Gwangju 108.8, Daejeon 108.6, Seoul 93.8. All these fell back 20–30 % by vol XII as the screening policy was re-calibrated. The cleanest public-health-intervention fingerprint in CI5. China is 5–10 years behind on the same trajectory. See [Thyroid](cancer.html#code=49).

title: Hawaii-White melanoma matches Queensland exactly
text: Queensland men 68.3, Hawaii-White men 44.2 — the latter up from 3.6 in vol I (n=19), a 12-fold climb. Same latitude + same skin type = same outcome; a single-registry "natural experiment" that punctures the "Australia is unique" narrative. Pop_warning on Hawaii vol VII, but the magnitude of the climb is robust to it. See [Melanoma of skin](cancer.html#code=24).
notes: SMALL_N id=684009999 sx=1 min cases=19; POP_WARN id=684009999 vol=7

title: The Zimbabwe Harare HIV-associated eye cancer inverted-U
text: African women 0.42 (vol VII, n=8) → 5.76 (vol XI, n≈43) → 2.89 (vol XII, n=82) — rise tracks the HIV epidemic, partial fall tracks ART rollout. Same pattern for Harare NHL men (n=55→347, much larger numerator). The eye-cancer numerator is small in vol VII; the NHL curve carries the statistical weight. An extraordinarily clean ART-impact visual. See [Eye](cancer.html#code=47), [Non-Hodgkin lymphoma](cancer.html#code=53).
notes: SMALL_N id=171600299 sx=1 min cases=13; SMALL_N id=171600299 sx=2 min cases=8

title: The Nordic stomach-cancer collapse
text: Iceland men 97.0 → 5.1 (−95 %); Finland 74.5 → 6.0 (−92 %); Norway 47.7 → 6.1 (−87 %); Denmark 40.6 → 7.1 (−83 %). One of the most complete cancer-burden disappearances ever recorded, and it would make a gorgeous four-line chart. See [Stomach](cancer.html#code=11).

title: Israel's gallbladder-cancer collapse
text: Israel women 11.4 → 1.6 (ASR), a 86 % fall on 307→524 cases. The Chilean comparator at 25–27 refers to Chile-Valdivia (id 215200199) rather than all of Chile. Two figures on one page — "high plateau" vs "disappearing excess" — tells an interesting pair of stories the literature has not unified. Pop_warning on Israel vol V; the collapse is large enough to survive it. See [Gallbladder etc.](cancer.html#code=17).
notes: SMALL_N id=215200099 sx=2 min cases=0; POP_WARN id=437600099 vol=5

title: Pacific-peoples' endometrial cancer now leads the world
text: NZ Pacific-peoples women vol XII ASR 52.5 — above every US subgroup. Obesity + parity story, but nobody is publishing on it. See [Corpus uteri](cancer.html#code=33).
notes: SMALL_N id=655400029 sx=2 min cases=14; POP_WARN id=655400029 vol=10

title: Hawaii-Japanese breast cancer has a uniquely late age peak
text: Peak at band 17 (75–79), ASR 147 in vol II. Most populations peak 10 years younger. Hawaii-Japanese vol I n=90, building to 1473 by vol XII. Pop_warning on Hawaii vol VII (the collapse-magnitude is credible but don't over-parse vol VI–VII). A quiet but publishable age-curve story. See [Female breast](cancer.html#code=29).

title: Singapore Indian women's "U-shape three-site combo"
text: Lip/oral 37.5 → 4.7 → 5.5; cervix 40.4 → 3.7 → 4.2; oesophagus 7.6 → 8.6 → 0.5. Three aerodigestive cancers tracking roughly the same curve in the same ethnic sub-population across 40 years. Suggests a single behavioural / cultural driver (betel chewing / tobacco). Ethnic-Indian case counts are small in early volumes (n=5–25 per site per vol); directions are robust but exact magnitudes are noisy. See [Lip, oral cavity](cancer.html#code=1), [Cervix uteri](cancer.html#code=32), [Oesophagus](cancer.html#code=10).
notes: DIVERGE id=470200040 sx=2 (ASR -32.05, crude +0.65); SMALL_N id=470200040 sx=2 min cases=12; SMALL_N id=470200040 sx=2 min cases=5

title: The African prostate-cancer rise is not a screening artefact
text: Uganda Kyadondo 5.74 → 53.0 (ASR, +823 %) on 16→594 cases; crude 2.22→9.37 (+323 %) — both rising. Pop_warning vol VII acknowledged. Rising in regions without widespread PSA. Genuine burden increase; very different story from the PSA-driven Western wave. See [Prostate](cancer.html#code=39).
notes: SMALL_N id=180000299 sx=1 min cases=16; POP_WARN id=180000299 vol=7

section: B — Strong known stories with a fresh angle
title: The persistent US Black × prostate excess
text: Detroit Black men 202.0 in vol VIII; ratio to US Native-American men 9–14× and barely narrowing. See [Prostate](cancer.html#code=39).

section: B — Strong known stories with a fresh angle
title: The persistent US Black × multiple-myeloma excess
text: SF Bay Black 11.64 in vol III, Nebraska Black women 12.19 in vol XII. Ratio still 12–17× in vol X. See [Multiple myeloma](cancer.html#code=55).

section: B — Strong known stories with a fresh angle
title: Chinese oesophagus / stomach belts (Cixian, Yangcheng, Yanting, Shexian)
text: Up to 97× median for Cixian women (oesophagus, n=1536) and 19× for Yangcheng men (stomach, n=1416). The double-burden geography tells a single pickled-vegetables / nitrosamines story. Crude rates have risen further in some of these counties even as ASR falls — the populations are ageing rapidly and part of the crude rise is that, not biology. See [Oesophagus](cancer.html#code=10), [Stomach](cancer.html#code=11).
notes: DIVERGE id=415601899 sx=1 (ASR -8.84, crude +9.83); DIVERGE id=415600699 sx=1 (ASR -3.82, crude +19.65); DIVERGE id=415600699 sx=2 (ASR -2.05, crude +6.32); DIVERGE id=415601899 sx=1 (ASR -11.70, crude +14.32)

section: B — Strong known stories with a fresh angle
title: Chile Valdivia gallbladder + testis outlier
text: Two cancers, same registry at the top — unusual and worth exploring. See [Gallbladder etc.](cancer.html#code=17), [Testis](cancer.html#code=40).

section: B — Strong known stories with a fresh angle
title: The Japanese "U-shape across five sites"
text: Miyagi / Osaka breast, colon, pancreas, corpus, other-female-genital all show the same low → lower → climb curve. One "Japan's cancer transition" figure could cover all of them. See [Female breast](cancer.html#code=29), [Colon](cancer.html#code=13), [Pancreas](cancer.html#code=18), [Corpus uteri](cancer.html#code=33), [Other female genital](cancer.html#code=36).

section: B — Strong known stories with a fresh angle
title: Australian Northern Territory Indigenous larynx and sinus signals
text: Indigenous women laryngeal ASR 5.20 (11× median) in vol XII, based on 7 cases — small but the direction matches the broader Indigenous tobacco + kava + HPV story. Nose/sinus numbers are too small (0–3 cases) to anchor a separate claim. See [Larynx](cancer.html#code=20).

section: C — Data-quality flags worth raising separately
title: India Trivandrum vol VII → VIII cliff appears in at least six sites
text: (pancreas, rectum, leukaemia, bladder, corpus, NHL). Strong signature of a scope change between the two volumes. A methodology note "what happens when registry scope changes" could use this.

section: C — Data-quality flags worth raising separately
title: France's U-shaped pancreas trends
text: (Isère, Tarn, Doubs, Hérault). Coordinated coding correction masquerading as a biological dip.

section: C — Data-quality flags worth raising separately
title: Qatar, French Polynesia, Brunei, Libya and Brazil (several registries) all have elevated "Uterus NOS" rates
text: a registry coding-quality flag.

section: C — Data-quality flags worth raising separately
title: Brain-and-CNS vol VI → VII step
text: triggered by the vol-VII reclassification of benign tumours; look out for any trend claim that straddles that boundary.

section: C — Data-quality flags worth raising separately
title: Finnish / Swedish "other endocrine" 1970s spike
text: almost certainly neuroblastoma being captured under C74/75 and later moved.

section: D — Registry / age-curve fingerprints worth a methods piece
title: UK cervix age peak at band 6 (25–34)
text: across all English regions — HPV-era signature.

section: D — Registry / age-curve fingerprints worth a methods piece
title: Malawi Blantyre paediatric Burkitt peak at age band 2 (5–9)
text: , ASR 42.4 for NHL men vol X.

section: D — Registry / age-curve fingerprints worth a methods piece
title: USA aggregate bimodal age curves for Hodgkin and leukaemia
text: at ≥ 18 000-case scale — a textbook visualisation of the "two peaks" pattern.

section: D — Registry / age-curve fingerprints worth a methods piece
title: Israeli Sephardic women childhood-brain peak at age band 1
text: possibly tied to post-WW2 tinea-capitis radiation cohorts.
All-sites aggregates (codes 62/63) were excluded by design.
Prostate and thyroid screening effects are covered per-site; no extra cross-site piece added.
Lung cancer's well-known story is already public; the detailed points are in [Lung](cancer.html#code=21).

title: Singapore Indian women follow a perfect U-shape
text: Vol III → X: 37.5 → 4.7 per 100 000, then rebound to 5.5 in vol XII. Same cohort, same registry, 85 % fall and partial recovery — exactly the kind of pattern the Shanghai cervix plot highlighted. The ASR in vol III (37.5) is 8.8× the global female median — tied for top spatial outlier in the dataset. Ethnic-Indian case counts are small (min 12 in a volume); crude rate is roughly flat, so the fall is genuine decline plus substantial age-structure change in the subgroup.
notes: DIVERGE id=415600399 sx=2 (ASR -3.30, crude +0.54); DIVERGE id=470200040 sx=2 (ASR -32.05, crude +0.65); SMALL_N id=470200040 sx=2 min cases=12

title: Canadian lip cancer disappearing
text: Newfoundland men fell 38.3 → 11.8 (−69 %), Saskatchewan 36.7 → 9.3 (−75 %), New Brunswick 34.7 → 11.3 (−68 %), all through a characteristic U (high-high-drop-bottom-small rebound). Plausibly fewer outdoor fishermen/farmers + UV protection — the lip vermilion component.

title: Inland-China intra-oral emergence
text: Jiashan County females ASR +93 % (n=26→71), Qidong +70 %, Harbin flat-to-down, Wuhan +5 % since vol VIII. Shanghai men's ASR fell 38 % but the crude rose 46 %, i.e. Shanghai's population aged rapidly — some of the cross-city comparison is population structure rather than biology.
notes: DIVERGE id=415600399 sx=1 (ASR -4.19, crude +4.42); DIVERGE id=415600399 sx=2 (ASR -3.30, crude +0.54); DIVERGE id=415601599 sx=2 (ASR -0.16, crude +0.63)

title: India & Pakistan dominate absolute levels
text: Kamrup Urban (Assam) males 59.5, Meghalaya 52.3, Pakistan South Karachi women 23.8 — all 5–6× the world median. Betel quid / gutkha belt.

title: USA ethnic gaps are enormous
text: In vol IV–V the US male top-to-bottom ratio across ethnicities was 27×; US vol V women had a 21× spread. The highest male subgroup was SF-Bay Black (ethnic 30), the lowest was Hispanic (42).

title: Bimodal age curves in Algeria and Jiashan
text: the female curve has a visible low-age hump before the usual 60+ peak, which is unusual for oral cancer.
None of the main findings ride on coding breaks for this site.
Uganda Kyadondo's +1548 % male change must be read with the vol-VII pop_warning — likely reflects population-denominator change.
notes: SMALL_N id=180000299 sx=1 min cases=4; POP_WARN id=180000299 vol=7

text: US ethnic subgroup spreads before vol VI should be read cautiously (small subgroup pops, early vol).
Document the Newfoundland/Saskatchewan/Malta lip collapse as a coherent Western-lip-vermilion story.
notes: DIVERGE id=312401299 sx=2 (ASR -3.17, crude +0.25); DIVERGE id=547000099 sx=2 (ASR -4.18, crude +0.55)

title: Cixian County, Hebei is in a class of its own
text: Female ASR 123 in vol VIII, 108 in vol X, 102 in vol XI — 97×, 93× and 88× the world female median respectively. No other cancer-registry combination in the dataset gets close to ~100× the median.

title: The Chinese oesophagus belt is visible across registries
text: Yangcheng County 85.5 (vol X), Yanting 69.4 (vol XI), Linzhou 61.9 (vol XI), Yanting 58.2 (vol XII). All among the top 10 spatial outliers in CI5.

title: Puerto Rico's collapse is one of the cleanest declines in the dataset
text: Female ASR 10.45 (vol I) → 0.50 (vol XII), a 95 % fall; men 23.6 → 3.7, 84 % down. Virtually no inflection — a monotonic historical disappearance.

title: Shanghai men: once 35.5, now 4.9
text: A −86 % fall from vol IV to vol XII. Women in Shanghai followed the same path (11.3 → 1.1, −90 %). This is the urbanisation mirror of the inland-China hotspots above.

title: Singapore Indian women collapsed through an inverted-U then down
text: 7.55 (vol III) → 8.63 (vol IV) → 0.45 (vol XII). 94 % down. Same Singapore-Indian slide also shows up in oral cavity and in cervix — the subgroup's whole upper-aerodigestive burden is rolling off. Case counts in the Indian subgroup are very small (5–8 per volume); Singapore-overall (n=150→96) confirms the direction.

title: Uganda Kyadondo women: ASR 1.16 → 12.98 (vol I → XII)
text: A 10-fold rise that earns an "erratic" tag because it jumps around. Worth checking against the vol-VII population warning before reading it as a real rise.
notes: SMALL_N id=180000299 sx=2 min cases=3; POP_WARN id=180000299 vol=7

text: None of the main findings ride on a coding break.
Uganda Kyadondo vol VII has a pop_warning; treat the magnitude of the Ugandan female rise with caution.
notes: SMALL_N id=180000299 sx=2 min cases=3; POP_WARN id=180000299 vol=7

text: The Chinese county registries (Cixian, Yangcheng, Yanting, Linzhou) are special-purpose cancer registries — their denominators are smaller rural populations and ASR is legitimate, but interpret as "pockets of exposure", not national rates.
Map Shanghai's 50-year descent alongside Cixian's persistence: a single country showing the full range. Squamous vs adeno split would strengthen the story.
Puerto Rico's 95 % female fall deserves its own short paper — what changed, and when did the knee occur?
Is the Ugandan rise real? If yes, it would contradict the general Sub-Saharan pattern and might reflect alcohol and very-hot-beverage exposure.

title: The Nordic collapse is near-total
text: Iceland men 97.0 → 5.1 (−95 %), Finland 74.5 → 6.0 (−92 %), Norway 47.7 → 6.1 (−87 %), Denmark 40.6 → 7.1 (−83 %). Women in the same countries dropped 86–92 %. Together these constitute one of the most complete disappearances of a cancer burden ever recorded.

title: The Chinese belt matches the oesophagus belt — same counties
text: Yangcheng males 168.9 (vol X), Shexian 151.9 (vol XI), Yanting 144.6 (vol XI), Cixian 118.0 (vol XI). 14–19× the world median. Three of these counties also top the oesophagus list — same rural diet signal (pickled vegetables, nitrosamines) hitting both sites.
notes: DIVERGE id=415600699 sx=1 (ASR -3.82, crude +19.65); DIVERGE id=415600699 sx=2 (ASR -2.05, crude +6.32); DIVERGE id=415601899 sx=1 (ASR -11.70, crude +14.32)

title: Hawaii Japanese shows the migrant inversion classic
text: Inverted-U: 61.2 (vol I, Japan-imported rates) → peak 66.1 (vol II) → 7.7 (vol XII). Their origin rates in Japan are still falling too, but the Hawaii curve falls *faster* — environment dominates genetics.

title: Ugandan rise
text: Kyadondo men 2.43 → 6.06 ASR (n=13→100) and women 1.08 → 4.55 (n=3→95). Small absolute numbers, and for men the crude rate actually falls slightly while ASR rises — direction is real but part of the signal is age-structure change. Pop_warning Uganda vol VII. Worth checking subsite (cardia vs non-cardia).
notes: DIVERGE id=180000299 sx=1 (ASR +3.63, crude -0.22); SMALL_N id=180000299 sx=1 min cases=13; SMALL_N id=180000299 sx=2 min cases=3; POP_WARN id=180000299 vol=7

text: None of these findings rely on a coding break.
The Iceland/Finland baselines in vol I (1955–1965) may include some oesophageal cardia — but the magnitude is large enough that the story survives.
A "global Nordic-collapse" figure comparing Iceland / Finland / Norway / Denmark on a single chart would be striking.
Why is the decline slower (or absent) in Chinese rural registries? Pickled-food penetration, *H. pylori* prevalence, refrigeration access — a geographic story waiting to be told.
Uganda: is the rise driven by cardia adenocarcinoma (obesity / reflux signal) or non-cardia (*H. pylori*)?

title: Almost no registry is falling
text: The "biggest decreases" panel is empty for both sexes — unusual in CI5.

title: US Black populations are climbing fast
text: Louisiana/New Orleans Black men 0.32 → 2.98 (+831 %, inverted-U shape peaking in vol XI); Connecticut Black men +855 %; Louisiana/New Orleans Black women 0.37 → 3.75 (+913 %). Minimum cell-counts are 1–2 cases in early volumes — the magnitudes of percent-change are exaggerated; statewide USA Black (SEER) shows a smaller but consistent rise.
notes: SMALL_N id=384000430 sx=1 min cases=2; SMALL_N id=384000430 sx=2 min cases=8; SMALL_N id=384000730 sx=1 min cases=1; SMALL_N id=384000730 sx=2 min cases=2

title: New Zealand Māori women have tripled
text: 0.28 (vol II) → 2.08 (vol XII), inverted-U peaking in vol X.

title: Ethnic spread inside the USA is 13×
text: Black populations at the top, Native American and Korean immigrant populations at the bottom.
Small absolute numbers; any single-registry trend should be read with confidence intervals.

Coding drift between carcinoid and adenocarcinoma over the CI5 era is a known issue — some of the rise may reflect better counting of neuroendocrine tumours (especially in registries with strong pathology). Worth flagging explicitly in any writeup.
The US Black steep rise: obesity/diet, or better histological capture of carcinoids? Stratifying by morphology in SEER-quality registries would help.

The universally male-heavy sex ratio in Europe deserves an explanation — Crohn's-related small-bowel adenocarcinoma? Note most specific registry claims (Poland Cracow 19:1 etc.) are based on single-digit female numerators.

title: Japan's inverted-U is perfect
text: Miyagi Prefecture men 5.45 → 37.90 (vol X) → 30.19; Hiroshima men 11.46 → 59.17 (vol VIII) → 31.44; women in Miyagi 5.95 → 24.02 → 22.0. Both the rise (diet westernisation) and the fall (screening + stabilisation) are in-frame.

title: Singapore Chinese men have overtaken Western rates
text: 2.86 (vol I) → 26.49 (vol IX) → 24.28 (vol XII). Women: 2.39 → 20.95 → 19.11. Clean inverted-U peaking around vol IX.

title: Kuwaiti rise from near-zero is extraordinary
text: Kuwaiti men 0.19 → 12.66 (+6500 %); women 0.76 → 11.73 (+1443 %). Even allowing for early under-ascertainment the magnitude dwarfs everything else.

title: Algeria / Israel Arabs mirror the Gulf pattern
text: Algeria Sétif men 0.73 (vol VI) → 5.48 (vol XI) in a U-shape; Israel Arabs men 5.46 → 19.86 (+264 %).
notes: SMALL_N id=101200199 sx=1 min cases=4; SMALL_N id=437600094 sx=1 min cases=15; POP_WARN id=437600099 vol=5

title: Low-baseline "floor" consists of African, rural-Indian and some Chinese registries
text: Uganda Kyadondo men 4.04, Langzhong 3.38, Mumbai 3.1 — around 0.2× the world median.
notes: SMALL_N id=180000299 sx=1 min cases=1; DIVERGE id=435600399 sx=1 (ASR -1.68, crude +1.59); POP_WARN id=180000299 vol=7

title: Sex-ratio curiosity in India Chennai (2.5:1 male) and Barshi (3.5:1 male)
text: Most registries sit near 1.3:1 — the Indian male excess is not universal across India.
None of the main findings are tied to a coding break for this site.
Kuwait's earliest CI5 period (vol V–VI) is thought to have had moderate under-ascertainment; the +6500 % magnitude is likely overstated but the direction and final level are robust.
Paper idea: "The migrant-catch-up and turn-around of colon cancer in East Asian cities — evidence from CI5 I–XII". Singapore, Hong Kong, Taipei, Osaka, Miyagi fit a single narrative.
Kuwait's story — from 0.2 to 12 in 40 years — is worth a standalone descriptive piece; the speed is newsworthy.

title: Japan again shows the inverted-U
text: Miyagi men 6.47 → 24.47 (vol X) → 22.49 (vol XII). More gentle than the colon curve — consistent with the idea that screening turns colon around faster than rectum.

title: India Trivandrum women: U-shape collapse then partial rebound
text: 22.0 (vol VII) → 1.77 (vol VIII) → 4.09 (vol XII). An 81 % fall then 130 % rise. Almost certainly reflects a registry-coding change between vol VII and VIII; flag for verification.

title: Female-heavy sex ratios in Africa/Caribbean are striking
text: Brazil Recife vol III M:F = 0.35 (women 3× men); Nigeria Ibadan 0.34; France Martinique 0.41; Uganda Kyadondo 0.39. Global vol-III median ratio is 1.5:1 male. Either a real biological signal or an ascertainment artefact (women more likely to reach registered care) — but consistent across multiple registries. Many of these ratios are computed from 4–20 female cases; treat as indicative, not publishable on their own.
notes: SMALL_N id=156600199 sx=1 min cases=6; SMALL_N id=156600199 sx=2 min cases=4; SMALL_N id=180000299 sx=1 min cases=4; DIVERGE id=180000299 sx=2 (ASR +1.61, crude -0.52); SMALL_N id=180000299 sx=2 min cases=9; DIVERGE id=207601599 sx=2 (ASR -3.28, crude +4.96); SMALL_N id=225009199 sx=1 min cases=5; SMALL_N id=225009199 sx=2 min cases=15; POP_WARN id=180000299 vol=7

title: Alaska Native and NWT women are the highest global outliers
text: Alaska women 165→242 cases, ASR 10.19→9.84 (flat); the 23.2 / 24.0 peaks refer to earlier volume IV figures for Native-only subsets. The headline level (3× world median) is real; the specific magnitudes depend on the subset and volume chosen.

title: Israel Jews born in Israel women: inverted-U with a vol-VII spike of 21.7
text: That vol is flagged in pop_warning for Israel — double-check before claiming a real peak.
notes: DIVERGE id=437600090 sx=2 (ASR -1.77, crude +3.80); SMALL_N id=437600091 sx=2 min cases=12; DIVERGE id=437600099 sx=2 (ASR -3.15, crude +2.10); POP_WARN id=437600099 vol=5

text: India Trivandrum vol VII vs VIII: the 22→1.77 step is suspicious; probably a scope change between the two volumes.
Israel pop_warning for vol V applies to the whole Israel file; the Israel-Jews-born-in-Israel vol-VII spike needs checking against the registry note.
notes: DIVERGE id=437600099 sx=1 (ASR -0.59, crude +5.13); DIVERGE id=437600099 sx=2 (ASR -3.15, crude +2.10); POP_WARN id=437600099 vol=5

text: Martinique 1975-era numbers have small denominators for ethnic/sex stratification.
notes: SMALL_N id=225009199 sx=1 min cases=5; SMALL_N id=225009199 sx=2 min cases=15

text: A "colon vs rectum" pair-plot across CI5 XI–XII to see which registries break the 2:1 colon-to-rectum rule, and why.
Explore the sub-Saharan / Caribbean female-heavy signal across multiple related sites (colon, rectum) — might indicate an entirely different aetiological contributor.
Alaska Native / NWT excess: is it right-side colon creeping into rectal codes, or a genuine rectal excess?
notes: DIVERGE id=384005099 sx=1 (ASR -0.37, crude +4.86); DIVERGE id=384005099 sx=2 (ASR -0.35, crude +2.88)

title: Thailand Khon Kaen is in a class of its own for fluke-driven cholangiocarcinoma
text: Male ASR 90.0 (vol VI), 97.4 (vol VII), 22× the world median; women 38.3–39.0 — also 21–24×. Persistently at these levels across five volumes.

title: Qidong City (male ASR 89.9 in vol VI) is the aflatoxin twin
text: same magnitude as Khon Kaen but from a very different cause. Worth showing both in one figure to highlight the "two 90s" with different aetiologies.
notes: DIVERGE id=415600299 sx=1 (ASR -42.45, crude +13.51); DIVERGE id=476400299 sx=1 (ASR -50.70, crude +10.18)

title: Mozambique Lourenço Marques, vol I (1956–60): male ASR 101.3, 35× the world median
text: This is the classic aflatoxin paper signal, still the single highest historical reading in the series. The age curve has a peak at age band 8 (35–44), i.e. ~30 years younger than the typical liver-cancer curve — extraordinary.

title: Osaka Prefecture men: 1.81 → 46.66 inverted-U, peaking vol VII (~1990)
text: This is the HCV-cohort wave washing through Japan — rose for decades, now declining. Singapore Chinese women are the female mirror (0.27 → 5.51, +1941 %).
notes: DIVERGE id=470200099 sx=1 (ASR -1.10, crude +12.76); DIVERGE id=470200099 sx=2 (ASR -0.02, crude +4.98)

title: Many Western countries show a creeping rise from very low baselines
text: Spain Navarra men 0.72 → 8.78 (+1119 %, n=8→215), Canada Alberta 0.60 → 7.02 (n=8→1069), India Mumbai men 0.55 → 6.84 (n=26→2060). Baselines are small (<10 cases) but finals are substantial. Part of this is genuine (HCV, NAFLD, migration) and part is better ascertainment of metastases-vs-primary.
notes: SMALL_N id=312400199 sx=1 min cases=8; SMALL_N id=572400499 sx=1 min cases=8

title: An Israel-Jews-born-in-Israel-men rise of +2640 %
text: from a 0.05 baseline is probably a coding artefact from vol II rather than a real cohort story — flag in any writeup.
Small and near-zero baselines in vol I–II inflate some percentage rises dramatically.
Historical liver cancer coding in CI5 included secondary liver tumours in some registries before vol VII — the "creeping rise" in the West may be partly artefactual.
Mozambique Lourenço Marques numbers come from a single volume (I); the magnitude is credible but cannot be tracked over time.

A side-by-side chart of Khon Kaen (fluke) + Mozambique / Qidong (aflatoxin) + Osaka / Singapore Chinese (HCV) + the Western creeping rise would illustrate a "four-aetiology, one-site" story elegantly.
notes: DIVERGE id=415600299 sx=1 (ASR -42.45, crude +13.51); DIVERGE id=415600299 sx=2 (ASR -7.82, crude +17.59); DIVERGE id=470200099 sx=1 (ASR -1.10, crude +12.76); DIVERGE id=470200099 sx=2 (ASR -0.02, crude +4.98); DIVERGE id=476400299 sx=1 (ASR -50.70, crude +10.18)

text: Early-peak age curve in Mozambique — under 40 — is striking and could anchor a piece on aflatoxin + HBV synergy.
The Israel-male rise needs a quick sanity check against the registry.

title: Chile remains the world capital
text: Valdivia women 27.3 (vol IX), 25.1 (X), 18.9 (XI); Bío Bío 24.6 (X). All 10–15× the world median. Reassuringly stable and well documented.

title: Israel's collapse is the biggest underreported finding here
text: Jewish women went 11.5 (vol II) → 1.4 (vol XI) — 87 % down. The whole-Israel line followed at 11.4 → 1.6. European-origin Israeli-Jewish women peaked at 22.8 in vol III and fell 86 % to 2.0 by vol VIII. This is on the same order as the Chilean excess — but falling.

title: USA New Mexico American-Indian women: ASR 31.4 (vol IV)
text: Third-highest global outlier, 9.4× median. An underappreciated population-specific hotspot — but the vol IV figure is based on a small Native-subpopulation numerator and predates the recoverable volume window for most cross-registry plots; pop_warning NM vol VIII.

title: Algeria Sétif: severe female-to-male skew
text: Women 3.94 vs men 0.74 (vol VI) → 9.99 vs 2.09 (vol IX), a 5:1 female:male ratio. Global vol-VI median is close to 1:1 the other way. Shared by a handful of North-African / Caribbean registries. Both sexes show ASR rising while crude falling — population ageing is part of the story.
notes: DIVERGE id=101200199 sx=1 (ASR +0.68, crude -0.07); DIVERGE id=101200199 sx=2 (ASR +0.88, crude -0.28)

title: Rising tide in East and South Asia
text: India Chennai men 0.15 → 1.82 (n=1→222); China Qidong women 0.44 → 2.71 (n=12→224); Singapore Chinese women 0.45 → 2.04 (n=14→312). Baselines are very small (≤14 cases in early volumes), percent changes are inflated; absolute final levels are modest but the direction is consistent across the region.
notes: SMALL_N id=415600299 sx=1 min cases=16; SMALL_N id=415600299 sx=2 min cases=11; SMALL_N id=435600499 sx=1 min cases=1; SMALL_N id=435600499 sx=2 min cases=2; SMALL_N id=470200020 sx=1 min cases=19; SMALL_N id=470200020 sx=2 min cases=14

title: Bimodal age curves absent; peaks sit in the usual 70+ window
text: **Bimodal age curves absent; peaks sit in the usual 70+ window.**

ICD reorganisation around gallbladder / extrahepatic bile duct / ampulla has moved codes between sites across ICD-8/9/10. Some of the Israel fall is likely to be real, but some may reflect reallocation of ampullary tumours to C24 → C17 boundaries; worth verifying against registry-level notes.
Chilean registries have had strong secular ascertainment improvements — level is credible, trend should be stated cautiously.

title: Why is gallbladder cancer falling so fast in Israel?
text: Classic risk factors (gallstones, obesity, parity, Mexican-Native/Mapuche genetics) don't obviously explain it. Early cholecystectomy rates plus change in obstetric exposures is one hypothesis.
Pairing Chile's level with Israel's fall would make a striking "high plateau vs disappearing excess" story.
notes: SMALL_N id=215200099 sx=1 min cases=0; SMALL_N id=215200099 sx=2 min cases=0; POP_WARN id=437600099 vol=5

text: North African female excess merits an investigation into radiographic-contrast history and obstetric factors.

title: Singapore Chinese: the cleanest inverted-catch-up
text: Women 0.27 (vol I) → 5.51 (vol XII), a 20-fold rise. Men 0.68 → 7.33 (+978 %). Almost monotonic — not clearly peaking yet.

title: French registries share a U-shape across sexes
text: Isère men 3.87 → 3.23 (bottom vol VI) → 10.28 (vol XII); Tarn men 3.70 → 3.36 → 9.35; Doubs women 3.85 → 1.85 → 7.71. The uniform shape suggests a vol V/VI under-coding issue that was later corrected — not a real biological dip. Flag for any trend-writeup.
notes: SMALL_N id=525000399 sx=1 min cases=15; SMALL_N id=525000399 sx=2 min cases=9

title: India Trivandrum men: 12.89 (vol VII) → 1.69 (vol VIII) → 2.81 (vol XII)
text: A single-volume cliff of the kind that almost always means a scope or denominator change in the registry.

title: Hawaii native Hawaiian women (vol II): ASR 18.7, 3.4× the world median
text: This is the top clean outlier (non-US-ethnic) for the site and deserves its own quick look — indigenous-Pacific pancreatic cancer is poorly described. Based on a single vol II reading in the native-Hawaiian subset; the aggregate Hawaii trend (n=41→600) shows ASR roughly flat while crude triples, i.e. much of the Hawaii-overall movement is population ageing.
notes: DIVERGE id=684009999 sx=2 (ASR -0.09, crude +12.96); POP_WARN id=684009999 vol=7

title: Biggest rises after Singapore Chinese come from Kuwaitis, Bangalore, and Chennai
text: a real convergence of Gulf and Indian urban rates toward OECD levels. Bangalore / Chennai baselines are very small (3–10 cases in early volumes); the direction is robust but the exact magnitudes are noisy.
notes: SMALL_N id=435600299 sx=1 min cases=6; SMALL_N id=435600299 sx=2 min cases=3; SMALL_N id=435600499 sx=1 min cases=10; SMALL_N id=435600499 sx=2 min cases=4; SMALL_N id=470200020 sx=2 min cases=11

text: No formal cancer_warning, but pancreas has had long-running diagnostic difficulty — a large share of cases historically registered as pancreas were actually peri-ampullary; the U-shape in French registries likely reflects this.
Trivandrum vol VII → VIII looks like a registry-level recoding, not biology.

A paper focused *just* on the East-Asian pancreas rise (Singapore, Chinese-American, Japan) would land well — the 20-fold climb in Singapore Chinese women is the single most under-reported magnitude in the dataset for this site.
Reverse-engineering the French U-shape would be a nice methods vignette on how coding reviews show up in CI5 trajectories.

title: Shanghai men fell 2.94 → 0.35 (−88 %)
text: n=63→131. The urbanisation + workplace-ventilation story familiar from other aerodigestive sites — one of the few registry trends on this site large enough to be statistically solid.

title: The generalised decline is near-universal
text: Most "biggest decrease" rows are near-zeros; many vol-XII female rates in US-Japanese and US-Chinese subgroups are literally 0.00. This is the signature of a disease class (wood-dust, leather-dust, nickel) that has been regulated out of most workplaces.

Numbers are small almost everywhere — many of the "rises" are from 0.05 to 0.5 per 100 000, so relative changes exaggerate. Italian provincial M:F ratios (Parma 63:1 etc.), Australian NT Indigenous rates, Iran Golestan female signals, Italy Sondrio +1000 % all sat on single-digit or zero-case cells and were cut from the headline findings on that basis.
The ICD-10 C30–C31 range excludes nasopharynx (C11). Don't conflate with the Asian-Chinese EBV-driven NPC signal.

title: Australia Northern Territory Indigenous women in vol XII: ASR 5.20, 11× world median
text: Based on 7 cases in vol XII — the magnitude is uncertain but the direction (female larynx cancer >10× above rest-of-world) is striking. Likely smoking + kava + possibly HPV synergy.
info: [C20|id=603600862|sx=2] vols 10->12  cases 1->7  ASR 1.42->5.20 (+266%)  CRU 0.63->3.87 (+513%)  Australia, Northern Territory: Indigenous || [C20|id=603600899|sx=2] vols 8->12  cases 3->10  ASR 1.21->1.58 (+31%)  CRU 0.71->1.71 (+141%)  Australia, Northern Territory
notes: SMALL_N id=603600862 sx=2 min cases=1; SMALL_N id=603600899 sx=2 min cases=1

title: Tobacco-era collapses are dramatic in ex-high-prevalence regions
text: Finland men 83.3 (vol I) — well, in larynx the cleanest one is India Mumbai men 18.2 → 3.7 (−79 %), India Poona 17.4 → 3.5 (−80 %), Hong Kong 15.1 → 2.9 (−81 %).

title: India Trivandrum shows the biggest male U-shape in the site
text: 78 (vol VII) → 9 (vol VIII) → 18 (vol XII). As with pancreas / rectum in the same registry, the vol VII → VIII step is likely a scope change.

title: US ethnic contrasts are enormous — and persistent
text: US male Black-to-Native-American ratio was 30× in vol VIII (17.3 vs 0.6); still 22× in vol XII (8.4 vs 0.4). This is among the largest persistent ethnic gradients in CI5.

title: Spain Zaragoza men in vol VII: ASR 17.1 vs women 0.09 — a 190:1 M:F ratio
text: Hungary Szabolcs-Szatmár 137:1; Spain Cuenca 111:1. These are among the highest male-to-female ratios ever recorded for any cancer site — a window into pre-1990 Mediterranean male-only smoking cultures. Female numerators are single-digit (≤5 cases in some cells); the ratios are arithmetic on those tiny denominators and should be read as a qualitative flag, not a quantitative estimate.
notes: SMALL_N id=221800299 sx=1 min cases=11; SMALL_N id=221800299 sx=2 min cases=3; SMALL_N id=572400799 sx=2 min cases=5; SMALL_N id=572401399 sx=2 min cases=0

title: Rising women in Spain, Norway, Algeria
text: (+350 to +530 %) — the late female smoking cohort catching up.

title: Qidong men 0.13 → 1.84 (+1315 %)
text: as with oral cavity and liver, this rural Chinese registry is catching up to urban levels.
No formal coding warning for larynx, but glottic/supraglottic split is inconsistent across registries — only rates should be compared.
Sex-ratio outliers in Spain / Hungary with female ASR < 0.1 are small-number sensitive; the 190:1 in Zaragoza is based on fewer than 5 female cases.
The Australian-Indigenous female excess deserves its own note — larynx cancer in women at this level is highly unusual globally.
The US Black-to-Native-American ratio has been published on before, but CI5's >60-year window shows that the gap has barely closed. A historical retrospective would land.
A "steepest Mediterranean male decline" companion piece (France, Spain, Italy) would complement the Nordic-stomach collapse piece.

title: Women peak (almost) nowhere yet
text: The "biggest female decreases" panel is empty. Canada Saskatchewan, Quebec, New Brunswick, Norway and others show female ASR multiplying 7–9× since vol I. Female lung cancer is the single most universally-rising indicator in the dataset.

title: Canadian Nunavut women, vol XI: ASR 97.5
text: 4.8× the world median, based on 41 cases in a single volume. New Zealand women 229→5549 cases (NZ-aggregate ASR 6.98→24.81, +255 %) confirm the broader Indigenous-heavy-country pattern. Canadian NWT women 72 (vol VIII). The Indigenous female lung-cancer burden is higher than almost any male burden anywhere today.
notes: DIVERGE id=655400099 sx=1 (ASR -24.43, crude +5.04); CODING_BREAK cancer=21

title: The male Finland inverted-U is textbook
text: 83.3 (vol I) → 100.8 peak (vol III) → 28.9 (vol XII), a 65 % fall from peak. USA White San Francisco men peaked at 86.8 (vol IV) and fell to 22.5 (vol XII), a 72 % fall from peak. The US Georgia Atlanta White men peaked at 92 and fell to 32.

title: Polish regions have the world's highest never-declined male levels
text: Warsaw City men 94 (vol IV), Cracow 101 (vol IV), Warsaw Rural 65 (vol VI), peaks in the mid-1970s. Decline has been slower than in most other European registries.

title: US Black-vs-Native male ratio 12× in vol IV and still 11× in vol XII
text: Among women, Black-vs-Native ratio peaked at 14× in vol V. Much more persistent than comparable gradients for tobacco-related cancers.

title: Extreme male:female ratios still exist in Muslim-majority registries
text: Turkey Edirne 15.5:1, Belarus 14:1, Mali 19:1, Tunisia Centre 21:1. These are the *least*-converged male:female ratios for lung cancer left in the world.

title: cancer_warning flags this site
text: ICD-7 was used for vols I–III, with some definitional discrepancies with later volumes. Don't place too much weight on the exact vol I–II levels — directionally they are fine but the magnitude should not be over-parsed. All lung findings above that span this break (notably Finland vol I, Canadian Saskatchewan vol I) inherit this caveat.
US bimodal "age-curves" in the scratch output reflect population-scale registries where ascertainment slightly differs — probably not a biological bimodality.
A single figure showing "women reaching men's historical peak" across 6 northern registries would be striking and publishable.
The Indigenous (Nunavut / NWT / Māori / Alaska Native) female lung-cancer excess is in need of a dedicated review piece.
notes: DIVERGE id=384005099 sx=2 (ASR -11.43, crude +6.40); CODING_BREAK cancer=21

text: Polish men have declined slowest in Europe — a focused analysis against smoking-prevalence and tobacco-control policy timing would inform ongoing debate.

title: Israel Jews born in Africa / Asia women vol V: ASR 6.49, 8.8× the world median
text: The top spatial outlier for this site by a wide margin. Israel aggregate n=38→255 cases, ASR ~1 throughout; the 6.49 reading refers to a narrower Sephardic subset in a single early volume and may not be fully auditable from the current CI5 2.0 plot. Pop_warning Israel vol V. Keep as a historical curiosity worth checking against the original vol V tables.

title: USA aggregate (SEER + NPCR) male age curves are bimodal
text: With the peak at age band 18 (85+). The low-age (adolescent) hump is visible but small relative to the chondrosarcoma/osteosarcoma-in-older-adults bulk. The USA bimodal pattern across vol X–XII may reflect how chondrosarcomas of the pelvis are being counted — large N (>6000 cases per vol) makes this easily visualisable.

Small numerators almost everywhere; rank bone findings by number of cases, not percentage change. Claims about Hawaii-Chinese men (n=0–9), Italian Ferrara/Sassari/Barletta M:F ratios (n=5–8), Karunagappally women U-shape (n=1→12), Xiangfu single-volume 5.88, and the USA-Black vs USA-other 10× historical gap all failed the ≥20-case threshold and were cut from the headline list.
The interpretation of "bone cancer" in CI5 mixes osteosarcoma, chondrosarcoma, Ewing, and (historically, in some registries) multiple myeloma — sub-type splits would be needed for a real writeup.
The bimodal US age curves are likely a registration artefact, not a biological signal.
Consider running all sarcoma-containing sites together (bone + soft-tissue under "other") to separate real geographic signal from coding drift.

title: Queensland men vol XII: ASR 68.3, 38× the world median
text: Queensland women 47.5 (33×). Tasmania women 35.3, Northern Territory non-Indigenous men 45.5. The Australian "skin cancer state" story is robust and intensifying.

title: Hawaii-White men caught up to Queensland
text: 3.59 (vol I) → 55.3 (vol X) → 44.2 (vol XII), a 12-fold rise in 50 years; Hawaii-White women also reached 36.9 (vol X). Same latitude + same light-skin denominator = same outcome.
notes: SMALL_N id=684009999 sx=1 min cases=19; SMALL_N id=684009999 sx=2 min cases=9; POP_WARN id=684009999 vol=7

title: Huge ethnic-within-country ratios
text: USA White vs Hispanic ratio 276× in vol XII (men 44.2 vs 0.16); White vs Korean 274× in vol IX; White vs Black women 208× in vol VII. These are the largest ethnic gradients in CI5 for any site.

title: European catch-up is steep
text: Malta men 0.6 → 8.6 (+1361 %); UK South West 1.5 → 16.0 (+995 %); Slovenia 1.6 → 17.1 (+950 %); France Somme 1.0 → 9.8 (+916 %). Women: Spain Zaragoza 0.3 → 4.2 (+1258 %); Switzerland Geneva 2.0 → 17.5 (+768 %).
notes: SMALL_N id=525000699 sx=1 min cases=7; SMALL_N id=547000099 sx=1 min cases=3; SMALL_N id=547000099 sx=2 min cases=7; SMALL_N id=572400799 sx=1 min cases=7; SMALL_N id=572400799 sx=2 min cases=7; SMALL_N id=575600299 sx=2 min cases=11; POP_WARN id=582600699 vol=8

title: Iceland's late female peak
text: Women 2.09 (vol I) → 19.0 peak (vol IX) → 10.6 (vol XII), inverted-U. Israeli women born in Israel peaked at 29.7 in vol VII then dropped sharply to 14.6 — consistent with sun-smart + better-screening hypotheses.
notes: SMALL_N id=535200099 sx=2 min cases=14; POP_WARN id=437600099 vol=5

title: Bimodal age curves show up in cooler regions
text: (Belarus, Tyrol, UK South West). The second peak sits at age band 14–17 (mid-60s), the first at the 30–40s peak. This is a clinically-reported melanoma signature (two modes) and CI5 captures it at registry scale.

title: Early female peak in UK Oxford Region vol III and USA Hawaii vol VII
text: melanoma peaks at age band 8 (35–44), which is exceptionally young and fits the pre-screening era.
notes: SMALL_N id=684009999 sx=2 min cases=9; POP_WARN id=684009999 vol=7

text: Historical US White vs non-White ratios of 200× depend on small numerator counts in non-White sub-populations — the ratios are real but ranking them on ratio alone overweights rare denominators.
A portion of the generalised rise reflects better biopsy/registry practices — direct comparison of vol I and vol XII should acknowledge that.

title: The Hawaii-White curve matches Queensland's almost exactly
text: a single chart of Queensland men + Hawaii-White men across CI5 volumes would be a beautiful visual proof that latitude + skin type drive melanoma, not "Australia as a special case".
notes: SMALL_N id=684009999 sx=1 min cases=19; POP_WARN id=684009999 vol=7

text: The female inverted-U in Iceland, Israel and Switzerland (peaking vol IX–X, now falling) is consistent with public-health intervention effects and is underreported.
notes: SMALL_N id=535200099 sx=2 min cases=14; POP_WARN id=437600099 vol=5

text: The US vol-XII White-to-Hispanic ratio (276×) is likely the largest *ethnic gradient* still active for any cancer globally.

title: The biggest absolute rate in the whole breast dataset: LA-County Filipina women, vol X, ASR 100.0
text: Still 83.2 in vol XII after some decline. This beats every US White and US Black subgroup.

title: Clean U-shape in Japan and migrant Japanese women
text: Miyagi Prefecture 15.3 (vol I) → 12.8 (vol II) → 74.6 (vol XII); Nagasaki 26.3 → 20.3 (vol V) → 56.4. Hawaii Filipina 25.0 (vol II) → 24.7 (vol III) → 85.9 (vol XII). All "U" because the first volume under-ascertained then the catch-up was steep.
notes: POP_WARN id=439200399 vol=7; POP_WARN id=684009999 vol=7

title: Singapore Chinese +785 % and Algeria Sétif +772 %
text: the catch-up is not limited to East-Asian migrants.

title: Extreme bimodal age curves in Hawaii Japanese women
text: Peak shifts to age band 17 (75–79) rather than the usual 13–14 (60–69). In vol II the peak was 147 per 100 000 at 75–79. This is highly unusual — most populations have a late-60s peak or earlier. Hawaii Japanese women have a *later* peak than anyone else.

title: India Karunagappally women: early peak at age 12 (55–59)
text: Notable because most breast-cancer peaks sit 10 years older; the premenopausal / perimenopausal hump in South Asian women has been described before but CI5 shows it here clearly with a large case count.

title: US ethnic contrast in vol V: 6.7× spread
text: (White 93.9 vs Korean 14.1). By vol XII the gap has closed to roughly 2.5× — but Filipina women *overshot* the US White rate to reach the top of the distribution. That inversion deserves its own note.

title: Uganda Kyadondo: 11.39 (vol I) → 32.93 (vol X) → 32.0 (vol XII)
text: Breast cancer in East Africa tripled in 60 years — the African epidemic story.
Breast screening roll-outs in different decades mean that a rising curve during vol VIII–X often captures a prevalence pool being worked through, not pure underlying incidence.
Hawaii Japanese late-peak numbers are small-N at band 17; sanity-check with larger aggregated data before making a story of it.

title: The LA-County Filipina rate (100.0) is the highest breast-cancer ASR in CI5
text: it beats every US White registry. Worth asking: acculturation effect, reproductive patterns, or registry differences?
The Japanese / migrant-Japanese U-shape: compare Miyagi with Hawaii Japanese across volumes to show the genetics-vs-environment separation.
African rise: Uganda Kyadondo, Algeria Sétif, Israel Arabs all share the same trajectory (+500 % to +770 %). A coordinated "breast cancer goes continental" piece writes itself.
notes: POP_WARN id=180000299 vol=7; POP_WARN id=437600099 vol=5

title: The classic U-shape in Singapore Indian women
text: 40.4 (vol III) → 3.7 (vol X) → 4.2 (vol XII). 89 % fall, now flat. Same U in New Brunswick (n=276→149), Hawaii Japanese (n=116→37), Hawaii Chinese (n=17→10), Geneva (n=112→58), Hawaii White (n=132→62). The shape is a cervical-cytology story: crashing rates then plateau. Singapore-Indian cases are small in early volumes (n=8–25); the U-shape in Hawaii-Chinese sits on single-digit counts in some vols — the combined signal across six populations makes the pattern robust even where individual subgroups are noisy.
notes: SMALL_N id=684009920 sx=2 min cases=8; POP_WARN id=684009999 vol=7

title: Uganda Kyadondo is the outlier where the rate kept rising
text: 24.8 (vol I) → 54.3 (vol X) → 48.0 (vol XII). Tripled and hasn't meaningfully retreated.

title: Sub-Saharan African registries are still the world's top
text: Zimbabwe Harare African women 86.7 (vol X) and 75.0 (vol XII); Zimbabwe Bulawayo 89.9 (vol XII); Malawi Blantyre 76.3. All 10–13× the world median. South Africa Eastern Cape 60.8. These registries carry the world's cervix burden in 2020s data.

title: Inland China is the most spectacular rise
text: Jiashan County 1.18 (vol VIII) → 9.89 (vol XII), +738 %. Qidong 3.69 → 13.78 (U-shape). Harbin Nangang 3.03 → 11.24. Wuhan 3.87 → 11.03. Zhongshan 3.54 → 9.98. Beijing 2.30 → 6.15. All rising, and **none of them fit the global "screening-driven fall" narrative** — these are cities where screening is still being rolled out and the underlying HPV-infection cohort wave has yet to peak. This is the story that justifies the whole project and the HTML presentation.
notes: SMALL_N id=415600799 sx=2 min cases=14; POP_WARN id=415600199 vol=10

title: Hawaii populations collapse together
text: Hawaiian -92 %, Chinese -87 %, Japanese -88 %, Filipino -90 %, Pacific peoples -90 %. One of the cleanest "big-cohort screening" effects anywhere.

title: Early peak in UK women across all sub-registries, vol XI–XII
text: Peak at age band 6 (25–34) in England, West Midlands, South West, East, South East — every large English registry. This is a striking early-age fingerprint that reflects the UK's earlier HPV-infection cohorts plus strong screening above 35 pushing the peak younger.

title: Bimodal age curves in the Netherlands and Denmark
text: a second peak in the 70s. This "screening-leaves-a-tail" pattern is known but CI5 vol VIII–X shows it with large case counts.
Hawaii populations have a pop_warning from vol VII; the magnitude of the collapse is credible but the exact trajectory inside volumes VI–VII should not be over-parsed.
Rising inland-Chinese registries should be checked against HPV-prevalence surveys; a portion of the rise may be ascertainment as registries mature.

title: A Shanghai-plus-inland-China figure showing the coastal fall and inland rise
text: is the core story the HTML hinted at. Add Wuhan and Harbin, and it becomes a paper.
The Sub-Saharan plateau at 50–90 ASR is a policy story, not a surprise — but CI5's 60-year window shows how long the excess has been sustained.
UK's age-peak compression to age band 6 is a clear HPV-era fingerprint that deserves a methods note.

title: New Zealand Pacific-peoples women, vol XII: ASR 52.5
text: Vol XI was 50.8. That's 4.5× the world median and the single largest endometrial-cancer rate in CI5 XII — above every US subgroup. Closely tied to BMI trends in Pacific populations.

title: Japan's catch-up is the steepest U-shape in the dataset
text: Osaka women 1.08 → 12.59 (+1066 %); Miyagi 2.33 → 12.97 (+457 %, U). Newfoundland women 0.70 → 20.39 (+2813 %) — the highest % increase, reflecting very low vol-I ascertainment plus genuine rise.

title: Asian migrant populations in the USA still lead the historical charts
text: LA-County Filipina women reached 21.6 in vol X (3× white US women at that point); SF-Bay Japanese women have been among top registries in every volume since vol III.

title: Singapore women went from near-zero to middling rates in all three ethnicities
text: (Chinese 4.17 → 17.07; Malay 4.76 → 17.49; Indian 3.41 → 17.52). U-shapes — genuine rise after a vol-IV dip that looks like a scope change.

title: US White vs "Asian/PI" ratios in vol IV were 13×; by vol XII only ~3–5×
text: One of the clearer cases of ethnic narrowing in the dataset.

title: China Qidong: 0.45 (vol VI) → 4.38 (vol XII), +873 %
text: n=14→252. Small vol VI baseline inflates the % change; the final level of 4.38 is modest but the direction is consistent with the same rural-catching-up-to-urban pattern we see for pancreas, colon, and breast.
Early-volume corpus-vs-cervix coding was sometimes blurred; some of the vol-I → II rise in Singapore reflects improving specification rather than pure biology.
Western-Europe registries had a known corpus-coding reclassification in the 1990s — small step-changes around vol VIII should be eyed carefully.

title: Pacific-peoples endometrial story
text: the world's top rate, yet barely discussed in global cancer literature. A focused note would land well in a journal with Pacific readership.
Japan's U-shape matches the breast, colon, and pancreas U-shapes in the same registries — could be bundled into a single "Japanese cancer transition" figure.
USA SF Alameda White women: ASR 47.1 in vol IV is one of the highest *early* rates for endometrial cancer; a historical note on post-war HRT + oestrogen-only era?
info: [C33|id=384010010|sx=2] vols 2->6  cases 441->659  ASR 21.18->22.14 (+5%)  CRU 23.77->31.13 (+31%)  Usa, California, Alameda County: White

title: Qatar (Qataris only) women, vol XI: ASR 10.4, 21× the world median
text: The single biggest outlier. This is not a biological story — it's a registry coding habit: cases are being left as uterus-NOS rather than split cervix vs corpus.

title: Other coding-driven outliers
text: Brazil Porto Alegre (9.0, vol VII), French Polynesia (8.3, vol VII), Libya Benghazi (8.3, vol X), Brazil Cuiaba (7.3, vol IX), Brunei Darussalam (6.5, vol XI). Treat any "unusual uterine cancer" paper on these countries with deep scepticism — it's usually an NOS artefact.
notes: SMALL_N id=409600099 sx=2 min cases=19; SMALL_N id=625009899 sx=2 min cases=15

title: China Yanting and Yucheng have elevated NOS rates (7+)
text: Unusual for Chinese registries, which tend to be well-specified — worth checking whether the rise is in incidence or in data-quality drift.

title: Almost universal decline in high-income registries
text: Several (Canada NWT, Canada Yukon, Hungary, Iceland) reach 0.00 by vol XII. Good news for data quality.
notes: SMALL_N id=312400999 sx=2 min cases=0; SMALL_N id=535200099 sx=2 min cases=0; POP_WARN id=312400999 vol=7

title: A handful of registries are rising
text: * — Switzerland Zurich +1033 %, USA New Mexico +644 %, China Beijing +458 %, Thailand Songkhla +622 %. Worth asking each registry whether this is a real increase or a coding drift in the other direction.
notes: SMALL_N id=384000999 sx=2 min cases=6; SMALL_N id=415600199 sx=2 min cases=19; SMALL_N id=476400499 sx=2 min cases=2; SMALL_N id=575600699 sx=2 min cases=2; POP_WARN id=384000999 vol=8; POP_WARN id=415600199 vol=10

text: Rates and ratios here are dominated by coding practice, not biology. Do NOT publish findings on "uterus NOS cancer" without reading the registry's coding manual.
Many registries with high NOS counts *may* also have depressed cervix or corpus counts — always check with cervix (code 32) and corpus (code 33) side by side.
A "registry data-quality snapshot" paper using NOS fractions as a quality flag would be useful IARC-internal work. Qatar, French Polynesia, Brunei, Libya, Brazil (several sites) stand out.
The rising Swiss and New Mexican NOS rates should be investigated — they're small but unusual because these registries are normally high-quality.

title: Japan Miyagi women, U-shape
text: 2.46 (vol I) → 2.17 (vol II) → 10.75 (vol XII). Biggest %-change at +337 %. The fall-then-rise is consistent with under-ascertainment + genuine reversal, not coding artefact (other Japanese cancers show the same shape).

title: Hawaii Chinese / LA Chinese women: deep U-shape
text: Hawaii Chinese peaked 16.18 (vol II) → 4.04 (vol XI) → 7.27 (vol XII). LA Chinese 13.64 (vol IV) → 6.82 (vol XII). 50 % fall with a weak rebound.
notes: DIVERGE id=684009920 sx=2 (ASR -8.91, crude +0.53); SMALL_N id=684009920 sx=2 min cases=7; POP_WARN id=684009999 vol=7

title: India Trivandrum: 19.50 (vol VII) → 5.10 (vol VIII) → 7.95 (vol XII)
text: As with other sites in this registry, the vol VII → VIII cliff is likely a scope change, not biology.

title: Inland China rising
text: Qidong 1.64 → 5.09 (+210 %), Jiashan 2.58 → 6.83 (+165 %). Worth looking separately at ovary vs vulva within this group.

title: Poland Cracow inverted-U peaking vol IX at 16.44
text: consistent with other Polish cancer peaks in the 1990s and the subsequent slower decline.
Aggregate site — the sub-components (ovary vs vulva vs vagina) have very different risk profiles. Any finding here must be re-investigated at the sub-site level to be publishable.
India Trivandrum vol VII–VIII step is almost certainly a registry coding issue.
Japan's U-shape for this aggregate mirrors breast, colon, corpus — this "Japanese cancer transition" across five female sites is a coherent story that deserves one chart.
Decompose the outliers into ovary / vulva / vagina using the sub-site version (C56 / C51-52 / C57) — the Pacific-peoples and inland-China signals likely differ.

title: USA New Mexico American-Indian women, vol III: ASR 8.71, 35× the world median
text: This is by far the top spatial outlier for the site — a historic finding and one that has not been revisited much in recent decades. Worth re-checking with subsequent SEER Native American subgroups.
notes: SMALL_N id=384000999 sx=2 min cases=2; POP_WARN id=384000999 vol=8

title: Vietnam Hanoi women, vol VII (1.85) and vol VIII (2.22): 20× median
text: The Southeast-Asian gestational-trophoblastic-disease belt is visible in CI5 with strong numbers.

title: Argentina Chaco (1.61), India Poona (2.68), Nigeria Ibadan (3.08)
text: all consistent with the historical geography of molar pregnancy.
notes: SMALL_N id=156600199 sx=2 min cases=0; SMALL_N id=435600699 sx=2 min cases=1

title: Early-age peak at age band 4–5 (15–24 yr)
text: in virtually every large registry — exactly where gestational trophoblastic disease is biologically expected.

title: No signals in men, which is as it should be
text: **No signals in men, which is as it should be.**
Very small case counts for almost every registry-volume cell; the magnitude of "top outlier" entries here is sensitive to one or two cases.
Gestational trophoblastic tumours have coding variability across ICD-8/9/10 — some may hide under C79 (metastatic) historically.
Most of the late-volume zero rates reflect genuine rarity plus small registries; do not over-interpret "collapse".
The Vietnam Hanoi + Argentina Chaco + Indian-subcontinent cluster maps the historical geography of hydatidiform mole well — a coherent descriptive piece could revive attention to the topic.

The US Native-American 1970s signal (New Mexico vol III) is interesting because of its extreme magnitude; a quick cross-check with the contemporary Native-American-specific SEER subset would reveal whether it persists.
notes: SMALL_N id=384000999 sx=2 min cases=2; POP_WARN id=384000999 vol=8

title: Brazil Recife men, vol III: ASR 9.54 and vol V: 7.94
text: roughly 10–11× the world median. One of the most striking historical South American rates.

title: Uganda Gulu and Zimbabwe Bulawayo African men sit above 5 in vol XII
text: African penile cancer remains ~10× world median (Zimbabwe Bulawayo African n=56 over the full period; Uganda Gulu single-volume n=21). Pop_warning for Bulawayo vol XII. Level is credible; Latin American registries have largely converged to global levels.
notes: SMALL_N id=171600170 sx=1 min cases=5; POP_WARN id=171600170 vol=12

title: China Shanghai and Hong Kong men: clean U-shapes
text: Shanghai 1.58 → 0 → 0.31, Hong Kong 1.95 → 0 → 0.37. Intermediate volumes have near-zero cases for these registries; the U is directionally suggestive but the bottom of the U is at the limit of what CI5 can resolve for this rare cancer. Improving hygiene / early circumcision increase during urban transition is the usual hypothesis.
notes: SMALL_N id=415600399 sx=1 min cases=0; SMALL_N id=415603099 sx=1 min cases=0

text: Small counts everywhere — treat all % changes cautiously.
CI5's penis code (C60) includes both invasive SCC and in-situ; some of the Swiss rise may reflect better capture of Tis.
Revisit Brazil Recife's 1970s–80s readings with an ethnographic lens: still one of the highest per-capita historical rates for any penile cancer population.

title: US Black men sit at the top of every volume
text: Peak prostate ASRs: Detroit Black 202.0 (vol VIII), SEER Black 185.4 (vol VIII), Atlanta Black 181.5, Connecticut Black 181.4, LA Black 176.3. The vol-X Black peak (Atlanta 206.7) is the highest site-specific ASR for any cancer in US data.

title: Asian catch-up is dramatic
text: Singapore Chinese men 0.81 → 33.81 (+4074 %), Shanghai 1.32 → 16.77, Osaka 4.55 → 40.50 (+790 %), Miyagi 5.41 → 39.95. Half the rise is PSA uptake; half is obesity + diet.

title: Newfoundland and Labrador men: inverted-U from 20 (vol I) to 95.5 (vol XI) and back to 75.8 (vol XII)
text: Canada's prostate wave in miniature.
info: [C39|id=312400899|sx=1] vols 1->12  cases 75->2163  ASR 20.20->75.80 (+275%)  CRU 12.46->165.39 (+1227%)  Canada, Newfoundland and Labrador

title: Spain and Israel-Arab men show the same inverted-U
notes: SMALL_N id=437600094 sx=1 min cases=17; POP_WARN id=437600099 vol=5

title: Algeria Sétif U-shape: 0.28 (vol VI) → 0.20 (vol VII) → 7.74 (vol XI)
text: Pure ascertainment pattern — the early volumes missed cases, then an active registry + PSA filled in the gap.

title: Uganda Kyadondo: 5.74 → 53.0 (+823 %)
text: African rise across the full CI5 window; not a screening artefact because PSA is much less prevalent. This is a genuine burden increase, plausibly driven by ageing population + westernising diet.
notes: SMALL_N id=180000299 sx=1 min cases=16; POP_WARN id=180000299 vol=7

title: US Black-to-White/Asian ratios 8–14× across volumes
text: and barely narrowing by vol XII (Black 149 vs Hispanic 15 = 9.7×). One of the most persistent ethnic gradients in oncology.

PSA screening entered clinical practice around 1987 (US) and over the early 1990s in Europe; any volume VII–IX rise in Western registries is at least partly attributable to prevalence-pool detection rather than underlying incidence.
No formal CI5 warning, but the direction of the "inverted-U" peaks aligns tightly with registry-specific PSA-screening uptake timing.
The Black male prostate excess across five decades is one of the most important persistent health disparities in US cancer data — a long-window CI5 chart would reframe it.
Uganda's rise (+823 %) alongside other African registries (Zimbabwe Harare, South Africa) is a genuine non-screening story that deserves its own paper.
Contrast Japan's pre-PSA (vol I–V) low rates, the post-PSA climb (VI–X), and the partial plateau (XI–XII) to reveal the "true" underlying incidence.

title: Swiss and Austrian alpine registries dominate vol XII
text: Graubünden+Glarus 14.4, Berne 12.6, Lucerne 12.0, Aargau 11.4, Carinthia 11.3. The "Alpine testis belt" is a known feature; CI5 XII confirms it has strengthened further.

title: Chile Valdivia men, vol XII: ASR 15.5 — the world's highest
text: 6.4× the world median. This is counter-intuitive (testis is normally a high-income-country cancer) and worth a closer look. Possibly reflects high early-life undescended-testis prevalence + catching-up through registry improvement.

title: US Puerto Rico: 0.45 (vol I) → 4.94 (vol XII), +998 %
text: n=7→403. Large vol XII numerator, small vol I baseline; the direction is unambiguous and the final level is solid, but the % change is inflated by the small baseline.

title: Huge ethnic contrast within USA
text: In vol IV, White vs Black ratio 27:1 (5.40 vs 0.20); vol XII Other-White vs Black 26:1 (8.04 vs 0.31). One of the most *widening* ethnic gradients in CI5 — the rest of the world rose but US Black testis cancer didn't.

title: The adolescent peak (age band 5 = 20–24) is in every registry
text: textbook.

title: China Taiwan vol VIII shows an early-peak at age band 1 (0–4)
text: probably yolk-sac tumours of infancy, a known pattern in East Asian cohorts.
Male-only cancer, no cancer_warning.
The generational rise is real but some of it reflects earlier detection of subclinical seminoma; historical registries of the 1960s almost certainly under-counted.
The Chile Valdivia outlier is worth investigating — the alpine Latin-American testis-cancer literature is thin.
The widening US White-vs-Black gap is an underreported disparity.
Map the Swiss Alpine cluster against the Austrian one and test for genuine biological continuity (altitude, oxidative-stress hypotheses exist but are speculative).

title: Everywhere is near-zero
text: Most registries fall to 0.00 by vol XII. The "biggest decreases" panel is dominated by registries with 0.27 → 0 transitions — more an artefact of rounding than a trend. Previously reported outliers (USA LA-Korean vol V 3.33; France Hérault, Korea Ulsan, Japan Saga rising +400 to +800 %) all sit on 1–15 cases per registry-volume and do not support a primary finding.
Aggregate category with very small counts. Do NOT publish findings on "other male genital cancer" without descending to actual morphology codes.
Historically, some C63 cases were coded under "unspecified urogenital" depending on registry — trend interpretation is essentially meaningless.
None as a primary story. Useful mainly as a *data-quality* flag — registries with persistently high C63 rates may have coding issues in prostate / testis / penis.

title: Canada NWT & Yukon women, vol IV: ASR 23.8 — 5.6× the world female median
text: Alaska n=89→249, Montana n=218→409, Oklahoma n=893→1770. The Indigenous excess is unusually prominent for this site and spans generations. Yukon vol IV sits on single-digit counts + a pop_warning (vol VII); the Alaska / Montana / Oklahoma aggregate numerators carry the statistical weight. Coding_break: kidney definition shifted between ICD-7 and later volumes, so vol IV → XII contrasts inherit that caveat.
notes: SMALL_N id=312400999 sx=2 min cases=1; CODING_BREAK cancer=42; POP_WARN id=312400999 vol=7

title: Japanese and US-Asian catch-ups: huge
text: Miyagi men 1.13 → 12.46 (+1003 %); Osaka men 2.92 → 11.76 (U-shape); LA Filipino men 1.11 → 9.98 (+799 %); Singapore Chinese men 1.32 → 10.44 (+691 %). Much of this is incidental CT detection; some is real.
notes: SMALL_N id=439200299 sx=1 min cases=12; CODING_BREAK cancer=42

title: Shanghai men: classic U-shape
text: 2.04 → 1.77 → 9.31. Characteristic urbanising-East-Asia pattern.

title: Spain Tarragona and Israel-Arab women: U-shape recoveries
text: 1.54 → 1.32 → 4.82 and 1.26 → 0.79 → 3.40. Consistent with a late-arriving CT-detection wave.

title: Morocco Casablanca women and India Chennai women show bimodal age curves
text: with a secondary peak at age band 14 (65–69). The shapes are unusual for kidney cancer and deserve a sanity-check — could be Wilms-tumour vs renal-cell confusion in older volumes.
notes: SMALL_N id=435600499 sx=2 min cases=4; CODING_BREAK cancer=42

title: Women-heavy registries
text: The Gambia 0.13 (M 0.14, F 1.04 — nearly 1:8 ratio, an exceptional inversion!), Barshi/Paranda 0.27, Northwest Territories + Yukon 0.36. Kidney cancer is usually 2:1 male:female; the Gambia's reversal is notable — but numerators are 3–6 cases per cell and NWT/Yukon numbers show ASR/crude divergence (population ageing). Treat as indicative, not quantitative.
notes: SMALL_N id=127000099 sx=1 min cases=3; SMALL_N id=127000099 sx=2 min cases=4; DIVERGE id=312400499 sx=1 (ASR -1.00, crude +5.82); SMALL_N id=312400499 sx=1 min cases=7; SMALL_N id=312400499 sx=2 min cases=2; SMALL_N id=312400999 sx=1 min cases=4; SMALL_N id=312400999 sx=2 min cases=1; CODING_BREAK cancer=42; POP_WARN id=312400999 vol=7

title: Ethnic contrast inside USA is 11–21× depending on volume
text: US White-to-Asian ratios were 21× in vol IV, around 10× in vol X — narrowing but still large.

title: cancer_warning
text: ICD-7 was used for the first three volumes, with some discrepancies versus later volumes. Don't read too much into the vol I–II → vol IV step.
Incidental-imaging detection (late-1980s onward) accounts for a substantial part of the Western rise; corrected-for-stage analyses in national data would separate real from apparent.
The Indigenous (Alaska, Canada NWT, Montana, Oklahoma) women's excess needs a focused review — the magnitude is striking and persistent.
A "kidney + bladder as a single urinary-tract story in urbanising East Asia" paper with Shanghai, Miyagi, Osaka, Singapore, Seoul would land well.
notes: SMALL_N id=439200299 sx=1 min cases=12; SMALL_N id=439200299 sx=2 min cases=15; CODING_BREAK cancer=42

text: The bimodal Moroccan and Indian kidney age-curves could reveal a coding issue with Wilms tumour at the adult boundary.

title: Zimbabwe Bulawayo African women, vol II: ASR 34.14, 8.3× the world median
text: A classic schistosomiasis signal. Based on 8 cases in vol II — magnitude credible at the order-of-magnitude level but not precisely. Mozambique Lourenço Marques women (13.53) also elevated. These are bilharzial squamous bladder cancers, not the urothelial cancer of smokers. Pop_warning Bulawayo vol XII + coding_break bladder inherit.
info: [C45|id=171600170|sx=2] vols 2->12  cases 8->17  ASR 34.14->1.71 (-95%)  CRU 2.86->0.90 (-68%)  Zimbabwe, Bulawayo: African
notes: SMALL_N id=171600170 sx=2 min cases=6; CODING_BREAK cancer=45; POP_WARN id=171600170 vol=12

title: Southern-Italian "Naples cluster" in vol XII
text: Naples Centre 42.2, Naples North 41.6, Naples South 41.5, Caserta 39.7. All around 3.8× the world median, all clustered. Navarra (Spain) 39.2 matches. Either tobacco-cohort persistence or industrial exposure — worth a separate look.
notes: DIVERGE id=538001699 sx=1 (ASR -5.76, crude +23.64); DIVERGE id=538004499 sx=2 (ASR -0.32, crude +0.81); CODING_BREAK cancer=45; POP_WARN id=538001699 vol=12

title: Algeria Sétif women, inverted-U
text: 0.07 → 2.50 (vol X) → 1.14 (n=2→24). Israel Arab women 0.22 → 3.18 (vol XI). Algeria baseline sits at 1–2 cases — the +1500 % headline is exaggerated by the tiny numerator; the direction is nevertheless one of the few rising female-bladder trajectories.
notes: SMALL_N id=101200199 sx=2 min cases=1; CODING_BREAK cancer=45; POP_WARN id=437600099 vol=5

title: Japan Miyagi and Osaka men: U-shape recovery
text: 6.12 → 5.48 → 15.61; 7.50 → 7.03 → 17.54. Either delayed tobacco-cohort signal or better endoscopic detection.

title: India Trivandrum men: 17.63 (vol VII) → 1.94 (vol VIII) → 4.94 (vol XII)
text: Same vol VII–VIII cliff as seen in multiple other sites in this registry — scope change, not biology.

title: Italy Ragusa vol V: M:F ratio 24:1
text: , Karunagappally 21:1, Hungary County Vas 18:1. Mediterranean male-smoking signatures.
notes: SMALL_N id=435600999 sx=1 min cases=9; CODING_BREAK cancer=45

title: Kidney (code 42) and bladder both show a Miyagi / Osaka / LA-Filipino cluster of U-shapes
text: Might be worth treating these two sites as a coordinated "urinary tract in urbanising East Asia" pair.

title: USA ethnic contrast persistent at 14×
text: in vol X (White 8.25 vs other 0.58 women).
notes: DIVERGE id=384000099 sx=2 (ASR -0.12, crude +0.55); CODING_BREAK cancer=45

title: cancer_warning
text: bladder may include non-invasive tumours. Any cross-registry level comparison must acknowledge this — registries with good urology services count Ta/Tis while others don't. The Italian Naples + Navarra cluster may partly reflect aggressive TURBT + Ta capture.
India Trivandrum vol VII–VIII is almost certainly a registry scope change.
notes: SMALL_N id=435600899 sx=2 min cases=11; CODING_BREAK cancer=45; POP_WARN id=435600899 vol=8

text: Zimbabwe Bulawayo vol II female rate is based on small numbers from a single 1960s volume — credible but cannot be tracked.

title: Naples bladder cluster in vol XII
text: deserves a standalone look: is it industrial (tanneries, dye), tobacco, or registration?

title: Schistosomiasis bladder cancer geography
text: Zimbabwe + Mozambique + Malawi in the 1960s–70s, paired with Egypt Gharbiah — though Egypt sits lower than expected in CI5, suggesting under-ascertainment.
The Algerian / Arab-Israeli women's rise is subtle but consistent.

title: Malawi Blantyre women, vol X: ASR 9.81, 20× the world median
text: By far the top spatial outlier. Malawi men vol X 7.43 (13×); Zimbabwe Harare women vol XI 5.76 (13×); Zimbabwe Bulawayo men vol XII 5.20 (13×). This is the OSSN (HIV-associated ocular surface squamous neoplasia) epidemic, visible in CI5.
notes: SMALL_N id=171600299 sx=1 min cases=13; SMALL_N id=171600299 sx=2 min cases=8

title: Zimbabwe Harare African women inverted-U
text: 0.42 (vol VII) → 5.76 (vol XI) → 2.89 (vol XII). The rise phase matches the HIV epidemic, the fall matches ART roll-out. Astonishingly clean.
notes: SMALL_N id=171600270 sx=2 min cases=8; SMALL_N id=171600299 sx=2 min cases=8

title: Bimodal age curves in most large US female populations
text: peak in older ages but a secondary bump in middle age. This is the mix of retinoblastoma (early band) and uveal melanoma (older) + OSSN, though OSSN dominates the adult low-age signal.

title: Japanese / Korean women: early-age peak at band 1 (0–4)
text: Retinoblastoma-dominant population distribution. India Tamil Nadu and USA Black women also have a band-1 peak, consistent with retinoblastoma.
Very small numbers for most registries. The African numbers are robust because they are large relative to the small Western denominators.
Eye cancer aggregates retinoblastoma, uveal melanoma, OSSN, orbital lymphoma — without histology split, cross-registry comparison is perilous.

title: The HIV-OSSN signal in Malawi / Zimbabwe is the most publishable finding on this site
text: Paired with HIV prevalence / ART timelines, the inverted-U in Harare African women could be a compact visual proof of ART impact on this cancer.
notes: SMALL_N id=171600270 sx=2 min cases=8; SMALL_N id=171600299 sx=2 min cases=8

text: The adult band-1 peak in Japanese / Korean women should be decomposed into retinoblastoma age breakdown — a clean paper on "retinoblastoma age distribution over the CI5 era" could come out of this.

title: Israel Jews born in Africa / Asia women, vol V: ASR 16.58, 4.1× the world median
text: Israel Jews born in Israel women also 16.05 in vol III. These are historical highs and consistent with reports of radiation-for-tinea-capitis treatment in North African immigrant cohorts (1940s–50s childhood exposure showing up decades later).

title: Inverted-U in Israel Jewish women
text: peaks vol III (16.05) and falls to 4.04 by vol VIII — 71 % down as the exposed cohorts pass.

title: India Trivandrum men: U-shape 11.62 → 2.44 → 2.85
text: Another registry where vol VII → VIII is a scope change, not biology.

title: Multiple registries show a "vol VI-peak and decline" pattern
text: USA LA Japanese men 3.06 → peak → 6.71 back to 6.71; USA NM American Indian men peaked 8.35 in vol III. Many of these sit near the benign-tumour coding boundary.
notes: DIVERGE id=384000099 sx=1 (ASR -0.05, crude +0.27); CODING_BREAK cancer=48

title: Rising African rates: Algeria Sétif +931 % women, +1375 % men; Uganda Kyadondo +583 % women
text: From tiny baselines but upward, consistent with improving imaging access. Baselines are 1–8 cases per volume (SMALL_N) and pop_warning Uganda vol VII; % changes are inflated by the small denominators. Direction is consistent, magnitudes are not precise.
notes: SMALL_N id=101200199 sx=1 min cases=6; SMALL_N id=101200199 sx=2 min cases=8; SMALL_N id=180000299 sx=1 min cases=2; SMALL_N id=180000299 sx=2 min cases=1; CODING_BREAK cancer=48; POP_WARN id=180000299 vol=7

title: Bimodal age curves in large US, Belarus and Croatia samples
text: childhood peak (band 1–4) + elderly peak (band 14+).
notes: DIVERGE id=519100099 sx=1 (ASR -1.30, crude +0.87); DIVERGE id=519100099 sx=2 (ASR -1.06, crude +1.41); CODING_BREAK cancer=48

title: Peak-age 1 in Israel Jews-born-in-Africa/Asia women
text: a childhood-tumour signal, possibly medulloblastoma-driven.

title: cancer_warning
text: brain and CNS may include benign tumours before vol VII. This is the single biggest interpretation hazard in the dataset — any "vol VI → vol VII drop" must be caveated.
Many registries changed imaging practice (CT, MRI) and coding at different times; cross-registry levels are not robust.
India Trivandrum vol VII → VIII step is registry-scope change.

title: Israel Jews-from-Africa/Asia women
text: a cohort paper on the declining brain-tumour burden from post-WW2 tinea-capitis irradiation. The numbers are big enough to support it.
A systematic vol-VI-to-VII comparison across registries would reveal which countries cleaned up benign tumour coding — a methods-heavy but useful IARC internal paper.

title: South Korea: Daegu women, vol XI, ASR 119.7. Gwangju 108.8. Daejeon 108.6. Ulsan 96.6. Seoul 93.8
text: Vol XI (2008–2012) was the screening-bubble peak. By vol XII the rates had fallen back (Daegu 87.3, Seoul 64.7) as the over-screening was reined in. Inverted-U shapes in virtually every Korean city. This is the single cleanest public-health intervention effect in CI5.

title: Chinese cities are rising fast — not yet past peak
text: Jiashan County women +4432 % (1.18 → 53.48); Harbin Nangang +2665 %; Wuhan +1834 %; Qidong (U-shape) 0.69 → 12.19. Men in Jiashan +2875 %, Beijing +1888 %, Wuhan +1575 %. The Chinese story looks exactly like the Korean one did at vol X — rise still ongoing.
notes: SMALL_N id=415600299 sx=1 min cases=3; SMALL_N id=415600299 sx=2 min cases=17; SMALL_N id=415600799 sx=1 min cases=7; SMALL_N id=415600799 sx=2 min cases=15; SMALL_N id=415601599 sx=1 min cases=18; POP_WARN id=415600199 vol=10

title: Italy Latina women: 1.55 → 31.83 peak → 29.66
text: a European microcosm of the Korean pattern. Italy generally is a high-and-rising thyroid country.

title: Sex ratio: thyroid is one of the few cancers where women outweigh men 3–5 to 1 almost everywhere
text: The strength of the imbalance is a thyroid-cancer fingerprint.

title: Early peak (age band 6–7, ~25–35 yrs) in many populations with high screening uptake
text: USA Iowa and Wisconsin women peak at 25–34, as do Trivandrum, several Chinese cities. This is papillary thyroid cancer being found incidentally in young women during other imaging.

title: US ethnic contrasts: 15× between Asian-Pacific and Native American groups
text: **US ethnic contrasts: 15× between Asian-Pacific and Native American groups.**

title: Hawaii Chinese women vol II: ASR 22.86, 7.6× the world median of 1970
text: An early, pre-CT outlier that has not received much attention. Based on ~21 cases in the Hawaii-Chinese subset — level is credible but the trend should not be parsed from vol II alone.
notes: SMALL_N id=684009920 sx=2 min cases=10; POP_WARN id=684009999 vol=7

title: Bimodal age curves in several Western registries
text: (LA Non-Hispanic White, Detroit White, Israel Jews). Two-peak distributions: one young, one old — reflects the papillary-vs-follicular histology split.

Most of the rise globally is *incidentalomas* — sub-clinical papillary cancers detected during ultrasound for unrelated reasons. Mortality from thyroid cancer has barely moved. Be explicit in any writeup.
No formal CI5 cancer_warning but the histology mix (papillary / follicular / medullary / anaplastic) is not stated; the screening signal is overwhelmingly papillary.

title: The Korean national-screening program's rise and fall is the single most striking epidemiological intervention signal in CI5
text: A standalone chart of Daegu / Gwangju / Daejeon / Ulsan / Seoul inverted-U would be the strongest plot the project can produce.

title: China is on the same trajectory 5–10 years behind Korea
text: a prospective comparison could warn policy makers that the Chinese inverted-U peak is still ahead.
The young-age peak (25–35) for papillary thyroid cancer is globally consistent; documenting the age-shift across vols I–XII tells the over-diagnosis story chronologically.

title: Sweden women, vol VI: ASR 6.56, 20× the world median
text: Vol IV 6.25 (18×); vol V 5.60 (16×); vol III 3.56 (9×). Swedish men in the same era 2.86–3.83 (8×). By vol VII the Swedish rates settle closer to the rest of Europe — suggesting a coding/aggregation change.

title: Italy Trieste women, vol VII: ASR 3.13, 8× median
text: Another small pocket of historical excess.

title: Early-age peak at age band 1 (0–4) in virtually every large US sample
text: this is neuroblastoma showing up clearly in the data, even under the "other endocrine" aggregate.

title: Bimodal age curves in US aggregate data
text: the paediatric (neuroblastoma) peak + older adrenal peak visible in large enough samples.
Sweden's elevated vol III–VI rates may reflect inclusion of neuroblastoma under C74 before reclassification — critical to verify before writing up.
Rates everywhere else are low enough that single-case swings drive the rankings.

title: Sweden's 1970s C74/75 spike
text: is worth investigating — was it a national screening / reporting initiative? Paediatric neuroblastoma in Sweden has been the subject of screening debates; this might be the incidence signature.
