Paediatric peak (band 1) + chromaffin tumour signatures would be clearer if the data were decomposed into C74 (adrenal) vs C75 (other endocrine); the aggregate makes some clean interpretations impossible.

title: Canada NWT & Yukon women, vol IV: ASR 8.0, 4× the world median
text: The top Hodgkin spatial outlier. Canadian Yukon men also flagged in vol VII at 7.95. Yukon numerators are single-digit in early volumes and carry pop_warning vol VII; treat the magnitude as indicative rather than precise.
notes: SMALL_N id=312400999 sx=1 min cases=2; SMALL_N id=312400999 sx=2 min cases=0; POP_WARN id=312400999 vol=7

title: US Nebraska Black women vol XII: ASR 5.79, 3.5× median
text: Based on 3→15 cases across vols X–XII; the ASR rise is direction-consistent with crude but the absolute numerator is small. Curious because the overall US Black rate is not especially elevated; worth checking the underlying subset.

title: Italian provinces (Ferrara, Benevento, Caserta) and Israel Jews-born-in-Israel women
text: sit at 5–6.5 ASR, 3× median. Italy's persistence of young-adult Hodgkin signals is well-documented.
notes: DIVERGE id=538001299 sx=2 (ASR +0.27, crude -0.39); SMALL_N id=538001299 sx=2 min cases=13; POP_WARN id=437600099 vol=5

title: Italy Latina men +273 %, France Doubs +321 %
text: (both inverted-U). Real trends not artefacts.
notes: SMALL_N id=525000399 sx=1 min cases=2; SMALL_N id=538000399 sx=1 min cases=10

title: Bimodal age curves in US aggregate data
text: peak at age bands 5 (20–24) and 16+ (75+) — the classic Hodgkin two-peak distribution, with a clear minimum at middle-age. CI5 visualises this fingerprint at scale (18–20 k cases).

title: Early-peak at age band 4 (15–19) in Russia St Petersburg, US Detroit, Quebec
text: the "young adult" peak dominant over the older-adult peak.
Hodgkin vs non-Hodgkin classification has shifted across ICD revisions; some small changes at volume boundaries reflect that.
Pediatric Hodgkin classification changes around ICD-9/10 may affect young-age rates.
The Canadian NWT Hodgkin excess is underreported — a brief Indigenous-health note could be written.
Italy's persistent young-adult Hodgkin signal is worth re-investigating in the context of EBV epidemiology and hygiene-hypothesis data.
Bimodal age curves at vol-XII scale would make a compelling single figure.

title: Zimbabwe Harare African men, inverted-U: 4.36 → 16.07 (vol XI) → 13.74
text: The rise phase tracks the AIDS epidemic; the partial retreat tracks ART rollout. Visually parallels the Harare eye-cancer curve — same mechanism.

title: Israel Jews-born-in-Israel women vol VII: 21.04, 3.3× median
text: One of the highest early NHL rates globally, well before the AIDS era.

title: India Trivandrum women vol VII: 19.14
text: Again paired with the vol VII → VIII scope change common to this registry — treat with caution but the vol-VII level is credible.

title: Nordic catch-ups: Finland women +354 %, Iceland women +356 %, Canada Newfoundland men +421 %
text: Most Western registries show 3–6× rises across the CI5 window.

title: Bimodal age curves in African registries
text: Nigeria Ibadan and Malawi Blantyre show an unusual double peak, with Malawi men having a peak at age band 2 (5–9). This is the Burkitt-lymphoma age distribution — NHL in sub-Saharan Africa includes a paediatric tail that the Western datasets don't have.

title: Malawi Blantyre men vol X, ASR 42.4 at age band 2
text: a paediatric Burkitt-lymphoma signal that hit a peak matched almost nowhere else in CI5.

title: Israel Jews-born-in-Africa/Asia men, age-band-1 peak at 40.7
text: probably a subset of cases in the paediatric range, consistent with early-onset EBV-related NHL in that cohort.

title: US Black-to-other-ethnic NHL ratios 5–8×
text: across volumes. Persistent, not narrowing.

title: Malta men +464 %; Singapore Chinese men +626 %; Canadian New Brunswick men +422 %
text: Western and Asian catches visible across volumes.
notes: SMALL_N id=312400599 sx=1 min cases=18; SMALL_N id=547000099 sx=1 min cases=12

text: NHL coding changed substantially between ICD-9 and ICD-10 (C82–85 + C96 captures most but not all lymphoid neoplasms); apparent vol VII–VIII rises may be partly classification.
Some HIV-related NHL cases may have been captured under other lymphoid sites historically.

title: The Zimbabwe Harare inverted-U is the clearest HIV/AIDS-cancer-epidemic fingerprint in CI5
text: Paired with Kaposi data (not included) it would tell a complete ART-impact story.
Malawi / Nigeria / Ugandan paediatric Burkitt peaks merit a descriptive update — CI5 XII numbers from Malawi are substantial.
The Israel-Jews-Israel vol-VII NHL peak is high enough to deserve its own look, likely tied to MALT / gastric lymphoma from H pylori (Israel has high H pylori prevalence historically).

title: USA San Francisco Bay Black men, vol III: ASR 11.64, 5× the world median
text: Nebraska Black women vol XII 12.19 (4.3×). New Zealand Māori men vol II 8.82. Multiple myeloma's Black-population excess shows up as early as vol III and is still at the top of US-male rankings in vol XII.
notes: DIVERGE id=384000230 sx=1 (ASR -0.62, crude +9.59); DIVERGE id=384000230 sx=2 (ASR -1.35, crude +6.89); SMALL_N id=384004730 sx=1 min cases=18; SMALL_N id=384004730 sx=2 min cases=8

title: USA ethnic contrast inside the same country reaches 26× in vol III
text: (SF Bay Black 11.64 vs Hispanic 0.44) — narrower but still 12–17× in vol X.

title: New Zealand Pacific peoples women: inverted-U 2.10 → 8.90 (vol XI) → 8.34
text: Pacific subgroup n=2→60 cases (SMALL_N baseline) + pop_warning NZ Pacific vol X. The inverted-U shape is directionally sound but the exact peak height should be treated cautiously. A Pacific-peoples myeloma excess that has not received much attention.
notes: SMALL_N id=655400029 sx=2 min cases=2; POP_WARN id=655400029 vol=10

title: The "universal rise" is the cleanest haematological trend in CI5
text: Virtually no registry shows a decline; most are up 2–6×. Extreme %-changes (Miyagi women +3125 %, Algeria Sétif men +1356 %) are driven by baselines of 1–4 cases; absolute final ASRs (2–5 per 100 000) are modest. The *direction* is universal and robust, but headline percentages should be reported with their baseline case counts.
notes: SMALL_N id=101200199 sx=1 min cases=2; SMALL_N id=101200199 sx=2 min cases=4; SMALL_N id=217000199 sx=1 min cases=2; SMALL_N id=217000199 sx=2 min cases=2; SMALL_N id=439200299 sx=1 min cases=4; SMALL_N id=439200299 sx=2 min cases=1; SMALL_N id=470200020 sx=1 min cases=7; SMALL_N id=470200020 sx=2 min cases=4

title: Uganda Kyadondo men 0.66 → 2.72, U-shape
text: African rise visible on top of the universal one.
notes: SMALL_N id=180000299 sx=1 min cases=2; POP_WARN id=180000299 vol=7

title: No cancer_warning, no bimodal or early-peak anomalies
text: myeloma's age distribution is a clean unimodal older-adult peak everywhere.
Much of the universal rise reflects better serum electrophoresis and bone-marrow biopsy rates over time. Some is real.
Small numbers in early volumes for non-US registries exaggerate % changes.

title: The persistent US Black × myeloma excess is arguably the most important persistent racial disparity in haematology, and CI5 XII shows it still at 12–15× — worth a focused paper
text: **The persistent US Black × myeloma excess is arguably the most important persistent racial disparity in haematology, and CI5 XII shows it still at 12–15× — worth a focused paper.**

title: The Pacific-peoples (Hawaiian, NZ Māori, NZ Pacific) myeloma excess
text: is under-described and could be packaged as a coherent indigenous-Pacific story.
Decompose the worldwide rise into a "diagnostic access" component (pre-vol-VIII) and a "real rise" component (post-vol-VIII) by fitting piecewise trends.

title: Zimbabwe Bulawayo African women vol II: ASR 42.52
text: extraordinary for 1960s-era African data. Almost certainly HIV-associated *before* HIV was named (lymphoid leukaemia / lymphoma overlap), but the magnitude is striking regardless. Sits as the #1 outlier for the site.
info: [C56|id=171600170|sx=2] vols 2->12  cases 7->43  ASR 42.52->3.18 (-93%)  CRU 2.50->2.29 (-9%)  Zimbabwe, Bulawayo: African
notes: SMALL_N id=171600170 sx=2 min cases=7; POP_WARN id=171600170 vol=12

title: India Trivandrum men: the by-now-familiar U-shape
text: 21.93 (vol VII) → 3.75 (vol VIII) → 5.46 (vol XII). Vol VII is real (it is the very highest Asian male leukaemia rate recorded); the dive to vol VIII is a scope change.

title: Kuwait non-Kuwaitis women vol IX: early-peak at age band 1 (0–4), ASR at peak 38.7
text: paediatric leukaemia signal elevated by the large migrant-worker demographics (young population skew).

title: Bimodal age curves in India Tamil Nadu, Korea, Hong Kong, Chinese cities
text: a clear paediatric peak (band 1–3) *and* an older-adult peak (band 14–17). This is the "two leukaemias" pattern (ALL in childhood + AML/CLL in late adulthood) showing up geographically wherever the registry is big enough to record both.
notes: DIVERGE id=415603099 sx=1 (ASR -0.33, crude +2.27); DIVERGE id=415603099 sx=2 (ASR -0.38, crude +0.31)

title: US ethnic contrasts 5× or less
text: unusually small for a cancer site. Leukaemia is one of the more ethnically balanced cancers in CI5.

title: France Calvados men: U-shape recovery
text: 4.31 → 3.27 → 13.29. Pronounced rise, U-shape with a significant rebound.
Leukaemia aggregates ALL + AML + CML + CLL — each with very different age patterns. Cross-registry comparison without sub-type split is limited.
Zimbabwe Bulawayo vol II (1963–67) is based on small absolute numbers; the ASR of 42 is credible but the magnitude should be stated with wide uncertainty.

The **Zimbabwe Bulawayo 1960s female leukaemia outlier** needs a historical retrospective — what was captured at Mpilo Hospital then, and is it really ALL/AML or a lymphoma-leukaemia misclassification?
A CI5-wide "two-peak fingerprint" visualisation (paediatric + old-age) would show how well large registries can capture both sub-populations and how many smaller registries cannot.
Kuwait non-Kuwaiti paediatric peak could be used to illustrate population-demography effects on crude age curves.
